dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: `adapters/pi/furrow.ts` registers a step-agnostic `tool_call` Write/Edit ownership handler that calls `runFurrowJson<ValidateOwnershipData>(root, ['validate','ownership','--path', filePath, '--json'], ctx.signal)`, and `decideOwnershipAction()` returns silent allow for `in_scope`/`not_applicable`, `{ block: false }` on confirm-yes/no-UI, and `{ block: true, reason }` on confirm-no; `bun test` passed with `26 pass, 0 fail`.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: `adapters/pi/furrow.test.ts` covers D5 gate interception plus all required verdict branches: `in_scope`, `not_applicable`, `out_of_scope` with confirm-yes, confirm-no, and no-UI fallback, and the new branching helpers in `adapters/pi/validate-actions.ts` all have direct tests.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: The implementation matches the D5 interface contract in `.furrow/rows/pre-write-validation-go-first/definition.yaml`: owned files include `adapters/pi/furrow.ts`, `adapters/pi/furrow.test.ts`, `adapters/pi/validate-actions.ts`, and `.furrow/rows/pre-write-validation-go-first/parity-verification.md`, and the parity file records the required 3 paired scenarios with step-agnostic Pi/Claude behavior.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: The deliverable’s touched files are within D5 ownership (`adapters/pi/furrow.ts`, `adapters/pi/furrow.test.ts`, `adapters/pi/validate-actions.ts`, `.furrow/rows/pre-write-validation-go-first/parity-verification.md`); no D5 changes outside those globs were required.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: The Pi-side logic is factored into small pure helpers with focused tests, follows the repo’s TypeScript/Bun setup, preserves handler ordering (`state-guard` before D4 before D5), and the Pi adapter test runner passes cleanly (`bun test` -> `26 pass, 0 fail`).
